tools.name: unulu_create_site
tools.description: Create a link-in-bio site with a name, bio, and links. Returns a live URL on unu.lu that expires in 1 hour unless claimed. Do NOT call until you have at least a name and one link from the user — gather real content first, never create with placeholder or empty content. If the user provides a handle

tools.name: unulu_get_state
tools.description: Returns the current state of a site including its name, bio, links, and URL. No authentication required. If expires_at is absent, the site has been claimed and is permanent. Accepts a site ID, a full URL (e.g. https://abc123.unu.lu), or a bare hostname (e.g. abc123.unu.lu).

tools.name: unulu_update_site
tools.description: Update an existing site. All fields are optional — only provided fields are changed. Links replace the entire array (omit to keep existing). Before updating, always call unulu_get_state first to read the current links and their ids — do not guess link ids. Authorization depends on site lifecycle: X-

tools.name: unulu_check_handle
tools.description: Check whether a handle is available on unu.lu (not other platforms like Instagram, TikTok, etc.). For example, checking 'joe' tells you if joe.unu.lu is available for claiming. Use this to help users choose a handle before they visit the claim page.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "unulu_create_site" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Create a link-in-bio site with a name, bio, and links. Returns a live URL on unu.lu that expires in 1 hour unless claimed. Do NOT call until you have at least a…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "unulu_create_site" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Create a link-in-bio site with a name, bio, and links. Returns a live URL on unu.lu that expires in 1 hour unless claimed. Do NOT call until you have at least a…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "unulu_update_site" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Update an existing site. All fields are optional — only provided fields are changed. Links replace the entire array (omit to keep existing). Before updating, al…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "unulu_update_site" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Update an existing site. All fields are optional — only provided fields are changed. Links replace the entire array (omit to keep existing). Before updating, al…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.unulu.ai" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: unulu_get_state, unulu_update_site · Private data: unulu_update_site · Public sink: unulu_create_site, unulu_update_site.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: unulu_get_state, unulu_update_site · Private data: unulu_update_site · Public sink: unulu_create_site, unulu_update_site

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
